_comment: FAITHFUL vehicle-breakdown / repair / weather magnitudes for The Campaign for North Africa (Phase 3, rules 21/22/29). Transcribed from the rulebook charts in docs/rules/90-charts-tables-and-play-aids.md and, where the OCR was garbled or dropped a column, RECOVERED directly from the scanned PDF (The Campaign for North Africa.pdf, chart booklet pages 6-8 = PDF pages 70/101/102/103/138). Mirrors data/logistics_rates.json: raw rulebook values, each block citing its exact chart/rule. DATA FILE ONLY -- no engine code reads it yet (Phase-3 Step 1). Scope = Fork B (faithful proxy): every real magnitude is here; capture/towing/destroyed-tank-recovery (22.4/22.44) and the TDS/Mobile-Repair specials (22.6/22.7) are DEFERRED and deliberately NOT transcribed. Two OCR corrections were made against the scan and are called out inline: (a) 8.37 Desert Breakdown Value = 24, NOT 2 (the long-assumed 'footnote-4 bleed' reading is wrong -- the scan shows two full-size baseline digits); (b) 21.38 column 41-50 has a 75%-row cell of 66 that the OCR dropped (so roll 66 there = 75%, and 64-65 correctly stays 50%). See faithfulness notes below.

terrain_breakdown_values_8_37._comment: [8.37] TERRAIN EFFECTS CHART, the 'Breakdown Value' column (docs/rules/90 lines 9-36; verified on PDF page 70). Breakdown Points (BP) a MOTORIZED vehicle accrues entering a hex / crossing a hexside. Foot/camel/motorcycle and inherent-transport guns never break down (21.11). Values are per hex entered or per hexside crossed. The engine will consume these beside movement.step_cost (they reuse the same road/track/escarpment branching).

hex_terrain._desert_note: VERIFIED on PDF page 70: the Desert cell reads '24' (two full-size, baseline-aligned digits), NOT '2' with a footnote-4 superscript. Contrast the same row's Mot CP cost '4-superscript-3' where the 3 is clearly a small raised footnote -- the breakdown '24' has no such formatting. Desert=24 is also the thematically correct reading: it is the single highest terrain value on the chart (double Mountain's 12), which is precisely what makes the open desert -- not the enemy -- the main tank-killer. The prior project assumption Desert=2 (from terrain.py's comment) is OVERTURNED by the scan.
hex_terrain._major_city_note: PDF page 70 shows Major City Breakdown Value = 1/2 (0.5); its non-Mot CP cost is 1. (An earlier note that had 'Major City 1' conflated the CP-cost column with the breakdown column.)
hex_terrain._absent_note: Swamp = may be entered only on road/railroad (no independent BV). Village/Bir/Oasis and Railroad = 'same as terrain in hex for all purposes' (BV inherited from the underlying hex). Fortification levels add no BV.

hexside._prohibited_note: 'P' = Prohibited to motorized units. Up Escarpment: a vehicle may not climb one (8.42), so it has no BV. Major River: crossable by a motorized unit ONLY where a road or railroad crosses the hexside, and then at NO Breakdown Points (note 11) -- hence no positive BV.
hexside._escarpment_note: Down Escarpment BV = 6 and is NOT halved by a track (the explicit exception in note 8).

road.note_6: A Road NEGATES all hexside terrain-feature entry costs AND Breakdown Point Values (note 6). During a Rainstorm a Road is treated as a Track for movement (29.56).

track.note_8: A Track HALVES all hex and hexside Breakdown Point Values (and terrain entry costs), EXCEPT the CP expended and the Breakdown acquired by moving a vehicle DOWN AN ESCARPMENT (note 8). A track carries no BV of its own ('-' on the chart).

light_truck_offroad_54_2._comment: [54.2] chart note (double-dagger): a Light Truck NOT moving along a Road garners ONE ADDITIONAL Breakdown Point per hex entered AND per hexside crossed, on top of the terrain/hexside BV.
light_truck_offroad_54_2.worked_example_54_2: The chart's own worked example: a light truck moving one hex along a track, across a wadi hexside, into a rough hex accumulates '1/2 x 8 (wadi) + 1/2 x 8 (rough) + 1 (wadi hexside off-road) + 1 (rough hex off-road)' = 4 + 4 + 1 + 1 = 10 BP. (The headline '18' in the OCR is an OCR corruption of '10'; the parenthetical arithmetic, which is unambiguous on the page, sums to 10 and confirms the track DOES halve both the hex and hexside BV.)

breakdown_table_21_38._comment: [21.38] BREAKDOWN TABLE (docs/rules/90 lines 1165-1178; RE-READ off PDF page 102 to recover a cell the OCR dropped). Roll two dice read as a two-digit sequential number 11..16,21..26,...,61..66 (36 outcomes). Pick the column by the vehicle's Accumulated Breakdown Points AFTER applying the BAR + weather column shifts (see 'notes'); the cell that contains the roll gives the % of that vehicle type that breaks down. Each column's cells partition all 36 rolls exactly (hand-verified). ROWS are the % results; each cell is an inclusive sequential range ('a-b' means a..b in the 11..66 sequence) or '-' (no outcome).
breakdown_table_21_38._ocr_correction_note: In column '41-50' the OCR (docs/rules/90 line 1176) omitted the 75%-row cell, leaving roll 66 uncovered (only 35 of 36 rolls). PDF page 102 clearly shows the 75% row as '- - - - - 66 66 65-66 64-66', i.e. column 41-50 DOES have a 75% result of 66. Therefore roll 66 in column 41-50 = 75% breakdown, and the 50%-row cell '64-65' correctly stays two values (64,65). This is the FAITHFUL fix; do NOT use the earlier proposed 'extend 64-65 to 64-66' patch (that would map roll 66 to 50% and is contradicted by the scan). All nine columns now partition all 36 rolls exactly.
breakdown_table_21_38.notes: % Breakdown = the percentage of that vehicle type that breaks down (round fractions per 21.35; a single TOE point ignores a 10% result). Column shifts applied BEFORE rolling and CUMULATIVE (21.3): the vehicle's BAR (right for +, left for -), Hot Weather = +1 column right, Sandstorm = +1 column right IF at least 50% of the accumulated BP were acquired in map sections containing Sandstorms. Motorcycles and inherent-transport artillery never break down.

broken_down_vehicle_repair_22_8._comment: [22.8] BROKEN DOWN VEHICLE REPAIR TABLE (docs/rules/90 lines 1208-1224; confirmed clean on PDF page 103). Roll one die (0-8; the die can exceed 6 via the Major-City modifiers below). Result by vehicle class and repair location. Truck cells: '2' = one Heavy Truck Point (or any Light/Medium combination equal to two) repaired; '1' = one Light or Medium Truck Point. AC/R cells = TOE Strength Points of armored car / recce repaired. Tank/SPA/TD and Facility cells = percentage of that vehicle type repaired (round fractions UP). 'na' = not allowed. '10%*' = if only a single TOE point is being repaired, treat as 0%.

die_modifiers._note: Subtract 1 for a Tank Delivery Squadron / Mobile Tank Repair Squad in the same hex (a deferred special). Add 1 (fort level 2) or 2 (fort level <=1) for a repair facility in a Major City hex.

broken_down_vehicle_repair_22_8._scope_note: Fork B uses only the two FIELD tank/truck/AC-recce columns each Repair Phase. The Temporary/Major Facility columns are transcribed for completeness/future use.

vehicle_repair_supply_costs_22_15._comment: [22.15] VEHICLE REPAIR SUPPLY COSTS CHART (docs/rules/90 lines 1180-1190; confirmed clean on PDF page 102). Supply Points expended BEFORE each repair attempt. Bd = Broken-down vehicle; De = Destroyed tank (DEFERRED under Fork B). AC = Armored Car; SPA = Self-propelled Artillery; TD = Tank Destroyer; Facility = Major or Minor Repair Facility. If a case is not listed, the repair may not be performed.

field_de_tank._deferred: destroyed-tank recovery is out of Fork-B scope

facility_de_tank._deferred: destroyed-tank recovery is out of Fork-B scope

weather_table_29_61._comment: [29.61] WEATHER TABLE -- RECOVERED from PDF page 101 (the docs/rules/90 OCR at lines 1045-1055 was incoherent: it scrambled the three multi-year Game-Turn sub-columns into the Normal and Hot dice columns, producing impossible cells like 49-60/97-108 and contradicting the 29.1 worked example). Each season row partitions the 2d6-sequential roll (11..66, 36 outcomes) among {normal, hot, sandstorm, rainstorm}. All four seasons partition all 36 rolls exactly (hand-verified), and the 29.1 example 'summer 53 -> Hot' now checks out (summer Hot = 24-55, and 53 is in range). A '-' = that weather never occurs that season. Sandstorm and Rainstorm are 'foul' -- proceed to the 29.7 Foul Weather Location Table for the affected map sections.

season_game_turns._comment: The Game-Turn column, read from the scan: each 48-turn year recurs the season. Year 3 is only partial (the campaign ends at Game-Turn 110).

foul_weather_location_29_7._comment: [29.7] FOUL WEATHER LOCATION TABLE (docs/rules/90 lines 1226-1232; confirmed clean on PDF page 103). After a Sandstorm/Rainstorm result on 29.61, roll one die for WHICH map sections the foul weather covers. Unaffected sections have Normal Weather. * = a Sandstorm on a (Nile) Delta hex is treated as Normal Weather. Sandstorms do not extend north of the coastal hexes.
foul_weather_location_29_7.die_3_delta_note: Die 3 -> sections D,E: a Sandstorm on a Delta hex in these sections is treated as Normal Weather (the '*').

bar_by_model._comment: Breakdown Adjustment Rating per tank/truck model, the recovered chart-of-record (mirror of logistics_rates.json fuel_consumption_rate_by_model). Signed convention: +N = N columns RIGHT on the [21.38] table (more breakdown), -N = N LEFT, 0 = none. The live seeded models also carry 'bar' in data/unit_stats.json; this block is the FULL transcription including models not yet in the OOB and the trucks. Sources cited per sub-block. Guns/AT/towed artillery are omitted: with inherent transport they never break down (21.11).

commonwealth_tank_4_47._comment: [4.47] COMMONWEALTH TANK & GUN CHARACTERISTICS CHART -- the BAR column did NOT survive OCR into docs/rules/90 (its rightmost surviving column is the Fuel Rate; the header collapsed 'Fuel Rate' / 'BAR' together). RECOVERED off PDF page 138 and row-aligned by cross-checking every Fuel Rate against logistics_rates.json (all 13 match). Note the texture: the unreliable British cruiser/infantry tanks (A-series, Crusaders, Matilda) are all +1R, while the American Lend-Lease tanks (Grant, Sherman, Stuart) and the Churchill are 0.
commonwealth_tank_4_47._sp_note: From the same chart's artillery/AT rows: SP 25-pounder ('Bishop') = +2R, 105mm SP Howitzer ('Priest') = 0, SP 6-pounder ('Deacon') = -1L.

italian_tank_4_48._comment: [4.48] ITALIAN TANK & GUN CHARACTERISTICS CHART (OCR'd cleanly, docs/rules/90 lines 3311-3341; spot-checked on scan).

german_tank_4_49._comment: [4.49] GERMAN TANK & GUN CHARACTERISTICS CHART (OCR'd cleanly, docs/rules/90 lines 3345-3380). All German tanks and SP guns = 0, BUT chart note *: every German tank has a BAR of +1R until the beginning of the 1/31 Game-Turn -- a TURN-GATE the reader must honor (not a flat value). Represented here as the post-1/31 steady value 0 with the gate recorded.

trucks_54_2._comment: [54.2] TRUCK CHARACTERISTICS CHART -- all truck points have BAR '2L' = -2 (two columns left). The Light Truck additionally accrues +1 BP per hex/hexside when moving off-road (handled in terrain_breakdown_values_8_37.light_truck_offroad_54_2, not as a BAR shift).
